input.prompt:
I have a solution with 3 MSTest 3.8.0 test projects that currently run on VSTest.
I want to switch all of them to Microsoft.Testing.Platform. We're on .NET 8 SDK
and use `dotnet test --logger trx` in CI. What changes do I need to make to each
project and to the CI command? I want properties set consistently across all projects.

input.initialPrompt:
I have a solution with 3 MSTest 3.8.0 test projects that currently run on VSTest.
I want to switch all of them to Microsoft.Testing.Platform. We're on .NET 8 SDK
and use `dotnet test --logger trx` in CI. What changes do I need to make to each
project and to the CI command? I want properties set consistently across all projects.

data.content:
## Migrating MSTest 3.8.0 to Microsoft.Testing.Platform

### 1. Add a `Directory.Build.props` (shared across all projects)

```xml
<Project>
  <PropertyGroup>
    <UseTestingPlatformRunner>true</UseTestingPlatformRunner>
    <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
    <EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
    <OutputType>Exe</OutputType>
  </PropertyGroup>
</Project>
```

- **`EnableMSTestRunner`** – activates the MSTest runner entry point.  
- **`OutputType Exe`** – required since each test project becomes a self-contained executable.  
- **`UseTestingPlatformRunner`** – routes `dotnet test` through the new platform.  
- **`TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport`** – enables the `dotnet test` ↔ new platform bridge (needed on .NET 8 SDK).

### 2. Each test `.csproj` – no changes needed

As long as they already reference `MSTest` (or `MSTest.TestFramework` + `MSTest.TestAdapter`) **≥ 3.8.0**, the runner is built-in. Just ensure no conflicting `<OutputType>` or `<EnableMSTestRunner>false` overrides exist.

### 3. CI command change

```bash
# Before
dotnet test --logger trx

# After
dotnet test --logger trx --results-directory ./TestResults
```

The command itself stays the same — `dotnet test` still works thanks to `TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport`. The TRX logger is built into MSTest runner ≥ 3.8, so no extra package is needed.

If you want to pass platform-native options directly (bypassing VSTest translation), use `--`:

```bash
dotnet test -- --report-trx --report-trx-filename Results.trx
```

### Summary of what each property does

| Property | Purpose |
|----------|---------|
| `EnableMSTestRunner` | Embeds the test runner in each project |
| `OutputType Exe` | Makes test assemblies executable |
| `UseTestingPlatformRunner` | Tells SDK to use new platform via `dotnet test` |
| `TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport` | Bridges `dotnet test` CLI to the new runner |

Place the `Directory.Build.props` at the solution root and all three projects pick up the settings consistently with zero per-project edits.
